title: CodeMirror editor component + file tabs
analysis:
Task 90 requires creating two SolidJS components: Editor.tsx (mounts CodeMirror 6 with Reify language support) and FileTabs.tsx (tab bar for open files). Both connect to the existing editorStore and bridge layer.

Existing infrastructure:
- gui/src/editor/reifyLanguage.ts: exports reifyLanguage() returning LanguageSupport with Lezer parser configured with folding, indentation, and closeBrackets config in languageData
- gui/src/editor/highlight.ts: exports reifyHighlighting (styleTags) wired into the parser via '@external propSource reifyHighlighting from ./highlight' in the Lezer grammar (line 205). The generated reifyParser.js imports and applies this automatically. So syntax highlighting is included when reifyLanguage() is used.
- gui/src/stores/editorStore.ts: createEditorStore() factory returning state (openFiles, activeFile, dirtyFiles, cursorPosition) and mutators (openFile, closeFile, setActiveFile, markDirty, markClean, setCursorPosition)
- gui/src/bridge.ts: updateSource(path, content), saveFile(path), openFile(path) wrapping Tauri invoke()
- gui/src/types.ts: FileData { path: string, content: string }

Installed CodeMirror packages: @codemirror/language (dep), @codemirror/state (dep), @codemirror/view (devDep). Missing: @codemirror/commands (needed for defaultKeymap, history, historyKeymap).

Testing approach: FileTabs is pure SolidJS testable with @solidjs/testing-library. Editor tests mock Tauri APIs via vi.mock following bridge.test.ts pattern. CM6 EditorView works in jsdom for basic operations. Tests create fresh editorStore instances per test.

Component design: Both components receive editorStore as prop for testability. Editor creates CM6 EditorView on mount with reifyLanguage, bracketMatching, syntaxHighlighting, history, keybindings. createEffect watches activeFile for file switching. FileTabs reads openFiles/activeFile/dirtyFiles from store.

Review feedback: Two blocking issues in gui/test/language.test.ts require fixes: (1) bracket-matching test line 86-88 has tautological assertion — the `|| openNode.name === '{'` fallback always passes since the node was found by matching `cursor.name === '{'`. Must import NodeProp from @lezer/common and assert NodeProp.closedBy directly. (2) auto-indent test lines 130-138 only re-checks language name/parser existence, never tests indentation. Must use getIndentation() from @codemirror/language with an EditorState to verify Block nodes produce non-zero indent.

prerequisites.description: Add @codemirror/commands to gui/package.json dependencies (provides defaultKeymap, history, historyKeymap essential for a functional editor with undo/redo). Run npm install to update package-lock.json.

Create gui/src/editor/Editor.module.css with styles: .container fills available space (flex: 1, overflow: hidden), dark background from --reify-editor-bg. Inner .cm-editor and .cm-scroller get height: 100%.

Create gui/src/editor/FileTabs.module.css with styles: .tabBar horizontal flex with --reify-surface background and bottom border. .tab button with padding, border-right separator, --reify-text-secondary color. .tab.active with --reify-accent bottom border and --reify-text color. .dirty dot indicator with --reify-warning color. .closeBtn small X button with hover state.

steps.description: Write failing tests for FileTabs in gui/src/__tests__/FileTabs.test.tsx: (1) renders tab for each open file with data-testid='file-tab', (2) shows filename basename not full path, (3) active tab has aria-selected='true', (4) dirty files show dirty indicator (data-testid='dirty-indicator'), (5) each tab has close button (data-testid='close-tab'), (6) clicking tab calls setActiveFile, (7) clicking close calls closeFile without switching tab. Use @solidjs/testing-library render/screen/fireEvent with fresh createEditorStore().

Implement FileTabs.tsx: SolidJS component receiving store prop. Renders div.tabBar with <For each={store.state.openFiles}> producing tab buttons. Each tab shows basename via path.split('/').pop(), sets aria-selected, conditionally renders dirty indicator span when path in dirtyFiles, includes close button. onClick tab calls setActiveFile(path). onClick close calls closeFile(path) with e.stopPropagation().

Write failing tests for Editor mounting in gui/src/__tests__/Editor.test.tsx: (1) renders container div with data-testid='editor-container', (2) container has .cm-editor child (CM6 mounted), (3) editor contains active file content. Mock @tauri-apps/api/core and @tauri-apps/api/event via vi.mock. Create editorStore, openFile with test FileData, render Editor.

Implement basic Editor.tsx: SolidJS component with store prop. Creates div ref container. onMount: finds active file, creates EditorState.create({ doc, extensions: [reifyLanguage(), bracketMatching(), syntaxHighlighting(defaultHighlightStyle), history(), keymap.of([...defaultKeymap, ...historyKeymap])] }), creates EditorView({ state, parent }). onCleanup destroys view. Returns div with data-testid and styles.

Write failing tests for doc change handling: (1) dispatching CM6 insert transaction causes store.markDirty to be called (dirty state tracks on edit), (2) after 300ms debounce, bridge.updateSource called with path and new content (use vi.useFakeTimers + advanceTimersByTime), (3) rapid edits collapse into single updateSource call.

Wire doc change in Editor.tsx: Add EditorView.updateListener to extensions. On docChanged: call store.markDirty(path), clearTimeout debounce timer, set new 300ms setTimeout calling bridge.updateSource(path, newContent). Store timer ref for cleanup in onCleanup.

Write failing tests for save: (1) triggering save calls bridge.saveFile with active file path, (2) save clears dirty flag (store.state.dirtyFiles no longer contains path). Test by accessing EditorView and invoking the save keybinding handler or by simulating Ctrl+S.

Wire Ctrl+S in Editor.tsx: Add { key: 'Mod-s', run: () => { bridge.saveFile(path); store.markClean(path); return true; }, preventDefault: true } to keymap. Returns true to stop propagation, preventDefault stops browser save dialog.
Write failing tests for cursor tracking: dispatching CM6 selection transaction to a known offset in a multi-line doc causes store.setCursorPosition to be called with correct line and column numbers.

Wire cursor tracking in Editor.tsx: In updateListener, when update.selectionSet: compute pos from selection.main.head, get line via state.doc.lineAt(pos), call store.setCursorPosition(line.number, pos - line.from).

Write failing tests for active file switching: (1) changing store.activeFile (via setActiveFile) updates editor document content to new file, (2) switching back restores original content. Render Editor with two files opened, verify content changes on tab switch.

Wire file switching in Editor.tsx: Add createEffect watching () => store.state.activeFile. When changed and view exists: find file in openFiles, dispatch transaction replacing entire doc (from: 0, to: doc.length, insert: newContent), set selection to 0. Track previous activeFile to avoid redundant updates.

Fix tautological bracket-matching test in gui/test/language.test.ts (lines 86-88). Replace the 'matches curly braces' test body: import NodeProp from @lezer/common, remove the tautological `|| openNode.name === '{'` fallback, and assert `expect(openNode.type.prop(NodeProp.closedBy)).toBeDefined()` directly. This ensures the test actually verifies that @detectDelim wired the closedBy NodeProp onto the '{' token type — a regression in bracket-matching configuration will now cause the test to fail. Verify the test passes with the current grammar (which does have @detectDelim).

Fix non-testing auto-indent test in gui/test/language.test.ts (lines 130-138). Replace the 'Block node uses delimited indent' test body: import EditorState from @codemirror/state and getIndentation from @codemirror/language, create an EditorState with doc 'structure S {\n  param x = 1mm\n}' and reifyLanguage() extension, call getIndentation(state, pos) where pos is the offset of the newline after '{' (i.e. the start of the second line), and assert the returned indent level is greater than 0. This verifies that the indentNodeProp configuration with delimitedIndent on Block nodes actually produces indentation. Remove the old assertions that only checked language.name and parser existence.

design_decisions.decision: Pass editorStore as a prop rather than using SolidJS context or a global singleton
design_decisions.rationale: Makes testing trivial (fresh store per test), avoids global mutable state. Parent component (App.tsx, wired in task 93) creates the store. Context can be added later if prop drilling becomes deep.
Editor without defaultKeymap and history (undo/redo) would be non-functional. This is the minimum additional package needed beyond what is already in package.json.

design_decisions.decision: Use EditorView.updateListener for doc changes and cursor tracking
design_decisions.rationale: updateListener is the idiomatic CM6 way to react to state changes. Provides docChanged and selectionSet flags, handling both concerns in a single extension.

design_decisions.decision: Debounce updateSource at 300ms but call markDirty immediately
design_decisions.rationale: Dirty indicator should respond instantly to user edits (UX feedback). Bridge calls to backend should be debounced to avoid flooding evaluation engine. 300ms matches task spec.

design_decisions.decision: No separate highlight wiring needed in Editor.tsx
design_decisions.rationale: Grammar file has '@external propSource reifyHighlighting from ./highlight' causing generated parser to import and apply styleTags automatically. Only need syntaxHighlighting(defaultHighlightStyle) for CSS rendering of tags.
Matches existing App.module.css pattern. Scoped class names prevent conflicts. Vite handles natively.

design_decisions.decision: FileTabs shows basename of file path
design_decisions.rationale: Tab bars show short filenames for readability. Full path can be tooltip. For .ri files, basename is most useful.

design_decisions.decision: Replace entire document on file switch rather than per-file EditorState
design_decisions.rationale: Simpler: one EditorView, swap doc via transaction. Per-file state preserves undo history but adds significant complexity. Can upgrade later.

design_decisions.decision: Fix bracket-matching test by importing NodeProp.closedBy from @lezer/common instead of using Symbol.for('closedBy')
design_decisions.rationale: Symbol.for('closedBy') is not the correct API — NodeProp uses its own internal storage, not global Symbols. NodeProp.closedBy is the canonical way to access this prop. Removing the tautological || fallback ensures the test actually fails when @detectDelim is missing from the grammar.

design_decisions.decision: Fix auto-indent test using getIndentation() from @codemirror/language with a real EditorState
design_decisions.rationale: getIndentation(state, pos) is the public CM6 API for querying indentation at a position. Creating an EditorState with the reifyLanguage extension and checking indent at the line after '{' verifies the full indentNodeProp → delimitedIndent pipeline end-to-end, without relying on implementation details.

reuse.how: Both components receive store as prop. Tests create fresh instances. Store provides openFile, closeFile, setActiveFile, markDirty, markClean, setCursorPosition.
Called in Editor.tsx as CM6 extension. Includes Lezer parser with folding, indentation, closeBrackets config, and syntax highlighting via @external propSource.
Editor.tsx imports updateSource for debounced doc changes, saveFile for Ctrl+S, openFile for loading file content.
Typing open file data ({ path, content }) in both components and tests.
Follow same patterns: vi.mock for Tauri APIs, render(() => <Component />) and screen.getByTestId() for component tests, vi.fn() for verifying calls.
Existing config handles SolidJS JSX and jsdom. No changes needed for new tests.

reuse.where: gui/node_modules/@lezer/common (already installed as transitive dep of @lezer/lr)
reuse.how: Import NodeProp in language.test.ts to access NodeProp.closedBy for the bracket-matching assertion fix.
Import getIndentation in language.test.ts to query indent level at a position in an EditorState, verifying the indentNodeProp configuration end-to-end.
